display.purpose: Hero numerals only — final scores, join codes, the countdown timer. Always tabular-nums.

headline.purpose: Question text during play, end-of-session leaderboard heading. Max width 65–75ch.

title.purpose: Stat values, leaderboard scores, secondary headings.

body.purpose: All paragraph text. Form inputs at 16px to prevent iOS auto-zoom.

label.purpose: Uppercase tracked section labels, status chips, metadata captions. The terminal voice.

mono.purpose: Geist Mono — typed input only. Form fields, join code entry, code identifiers. Never display digits.

motion.name: duration-instant
motion.purpose: Immediate feedback — hover border colour changes.

motion.name: duration-base
motion.purpose: Standard UI transitions — the default cadence.

motion.name: ease-out
motion.purpose: Expo-out — elements settling onto stage (entering, opening).

motion.name: spring-leaderboard
motion.purpose: Framer spring for leaderboard reordering — confident snap, no bounce.

motion.name: spring-gentle-counter
motion.purpose: Counter animations — points awarded, score climbing.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single CTA on a surface — broadcast-blue rectangle, uppercase tracked label, sharp corners.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; background: var(--color-primary, #2563eb); color: #ffffff; padding: 1rem 2rem; font-family: 'Geist Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.1em; text-transform: uppercase; border: none; border-radius: 0; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 150ms ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: var(--color-primary-hover, #1d4ed8); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 2px var(--color-primary, #2563eb); } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.4; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Secondary action — transparent fill, hairline Rule Line border, Quiet Slate text shifting to On-Air Sky on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; background: transparent; border: 1px solid var(--color-border, #1a1f2e); color: var(--color-muted, #94a3b8); padding: 1rem 2rem; font-family: 'Geist Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.1em; text-transform: uppercase; cursor: pointer; transition: border-color 150ms ease, color 150ms ease; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { border-color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--color-primary, #2563eb) 50%, transparent); color: var(--color-accent, #38bdf8); } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 2px var(--color-primary, #2563eb); }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Single-line text field — Booth Charcoal fill, hairline border, sharp corners, 16px font (prevents iOS auto-zoom). Focus doubles the stroke in Broadcast Blue without an outer ring.
components.css: .ds-input { width: 100%; box-sizing: border-box; background: var(--color-surface, #0f1117); border: 1px solid var(--color-border, #1a1f2e); color: var(--color-foreground, #f8fafc); padding: 0.75rem 1rem; font-family: 'Geist Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; outline: none; border-radius: 0; transition: border-color 150ms ease, box-shadow 150ms ease; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--color-muted, #94a3b8) 30%, transparent); } .ds-input:focus { border-color: var(--color-primary, #2563eb); box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 1px var(--color-primary, #2563eb); } .ds-input:disabled { opacity: 0.5; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Surface Card
components.description: Bordered container for grouped content — one tonal step above the page, 1px Rule Line border, sharp corners, 1.5rem padding. Never nest.
components.html:
QUESTION 3 OF 12

Which planet is the largest in our solar system?
components.css: .ds-card { background: var(--color-surface, #0f1117); border: 1px solid var(--color-border, #1a1f2e); border-radius: 0; padding: 1.5rem; font-family: 'Geist Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; color: var(--color-foreground, #f8fafc); } .ds-card-label { margin: 0 0 0.75rem 0; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.1em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--color-muted, #94a3b8); font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; } .ds-card-title { margin: 0; font-size: 1.5rem; font-weight: 700; line-height: 1.3; }

components.name: Interactive Row
components.description: Clickable list row — Booth Charcoal background, hairline border that warms toward Broadcast Blue on hover. Used for event/quiz/session lists.
components.html: Quarterly Town Hall Trivia3 QUIZZES · MAY 14
components.css: .ds-row { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; padding: 1rem 1.5rem; background: var(--color-surface, #0f1117); border: 1px solid var(--color-border, #1a1f2e); border-radius: 0; color: var(--color-foreground, #f8fafc); text-decoration: none; font-family: 'Geist Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; transition: border-color 150ms ease, background-color 150ms ease; } .ds-row:hover { border-color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--color-primary, #2563eb) 40%, transparent); } .ds-row-title { font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 500; } .ds-row-meta { font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.1em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--color-muted, #94a3b8); font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; }

components.name: Eyebrow Label
components.description: The terminal voice — uppercase tracked 12px label in Quiet Slate. Used for section headers, status chips, metadata captions.
components.css: .ds-label { display: inline-block; font-family: 'Geist Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1; letter-spacing: 0.1em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--color-muted, #94a3b8); font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; }

components.name: Status Chip (Accent)
components.description: Hairline-bordered status badge — no fill, tone colours the stroke and text. Filled chips are forbidden by the Spotlight Rule.
components.css: .ds-chip-accent { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.4rem; padding: 0.35rem 0.65rem; border: 1px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--color-accent, #38bdf8) 50%, transparent); background: transparent; color: var(--color-accent, #38bdf8); font-family: 'Geist Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.1em; text-transform: uppercase; border-radius: 0; }

components.name: Live Dot
components.description: Pulsing status indicator — a 0.5rem coloured disc animated via pulse-dot. Reserved for genuinely-live state; static dots are forbidden.
components.css: .ds-live-dot { display: inline-block; width: 0.5rem; height: 0.5rem; border-radius: 9999px; background: var(--color-accent, #38bdf8); animation: ds-pulse-dot 1.5s ease-in-out infinite; } @keyframes ds-pulse-dot { 0%, 100% { opacity: 1; transform: scale(1); } 50% { opacity: 0.5; transform: scale(1.3); } } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-live-dot { animation: none; } }

components.name: Display Numeral (Timer/Score)
components.description: The signature hero numeral — Geist Sans 900, tabular-nums, fluid clamp. Used for timers, scores, the join code on the host screen.
components.css: .ds-display { display: inline-block; font-family: 'Geist Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 900; font-size: clamp(2.25rem, 8vw, 5rem); line-height: 1; color: var(--color-foreground, #f8fafc); font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; }

components.name: Answer Option Tile (Option B)
components.description: Signature interactive — sharp rectangle, letter badge in the option colour, large readable text. Used during play and on host displays.
components.css: .ds-option { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 1rem; width: 100%; background: var(--color-surface, #0f1117); border: 1px solid var(--color-border, #1a1f2e); border-radius: 0; padding: 1rem 1.25rem; color: var(--color-foreground, #f8fafc); font-family: 'Geist Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1.125rem; font-weight: 600; text-align: left; cursor: pointer; transition: border-color 150ms ease; } .ds-option:hover { border-color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--color-option-b, #7c3aed) 60%, transparent); } .ds-option-letter { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 2rem; height: 2rem; background: var(--color-option-b, #7c3aed); color: #ffffff; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.1em; } .ds-option-text { font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 500; }

narrative.overview: Hermes is a live broadcast staged in a near-black room. The surround stays flat and silent so the question of the moment — the timer, the answer, the leaderboard delta — can step into the spotlight unchallenged. The atmosphere is theatrical and precise at the same time: a terminal's clarity wearing a broadcaster's stakes. Every element earns its colour because the room behind it doesn't compete. This system rejects four families by name: the candy-coloured carnival of Kahoot, the rounded-card soft-gradient politeness of generic SaaS dashboards, retro CRT kitsch (scanlines, phosphor bloom, fake noise), and crypto/gaming neon (cyan-on-black, glowing borders, gradient text). The terminal is the soul — monospace inputs, uppercase tracked labels, hairline borders — never a costume.
keyCharacteristics: Flat near-black canvas (#0a0a0f), one step lighter for surfaces (#0f1117). No gradients, no blooms, no glassmorphism.
Sharp corners as foundational identity. The radius scale exists for completeness; production UI uses 0 almost everywhere.
One accent at a time — the spotlight metaphor. Signal-white text, quiet-slate context, broadcast-blue for action, on-air sky for liveness.
Geist Sans for everything visible; Geist Mono reserved strictly for typed user input.
tabular-nums mandatory on every live-updating digit (timers, scores, counts) — the room must not jitter.
Motion is choreography, never decoration. Enter animations carry meaning (a new question arrives, an answer locks, the reveal lands).

rules.name: The Spotlight Rule
rules.body: At any moment, exactly one colour is permitted to act. Everything else is neutral, muted, or status. If two saturated colours are competing for attention in the same view, the design is wrong — pick the one that is on-air and quiet the other.

rules.name: The No-Tint-on-White Rule
rules.body: There is no white surface in Hermes. Text appears on near-black; primary actions appear on near-black; cards sit on near-black. Any urge to drop a panel onto white means the design has slipped out of the broadcast room.

rules.name: The Option Palette Is Sacred
rules.body: A/B/C/D colours mean 'answer choice.' They do not appear in chrome, in marketing surfaces, in icons, or in status messaging. Misusing them dissolves the most important affordance in the product.

rules.name: The Tabular Digit Rule
rules.body: Every numeric value that updates live — timers, scores, counts, ranks, participant tallies — uses font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums. The room must not breathe sideways when a digit ticks.

rules.name: The Mono Stays in the Field Rule
rules.body: Geist Mono lives inside form inputs, code badges, and typed identifiers (join codes, emails). It does NOT appear in headings, display numerals, scores, or labels. Geist Mono is not a display face and breaks at large sizes.

rules.name: The Uppercase Tracked Voice
rules.body: Section labels, status chips, button CTAs, and metadata captions are uppercase with 0.1em letter-spacing. Body and headline text are sentence case. The two registers stay separate — never mix uppercase-tracked with sentence-case in the same atom.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces never cast shadows. If a panel feels like it needs depth, the issue is contrast, border, or grouping — solve it there, not with box-shadow. Drop shadows are a tell that the design has reverted to SaaS-dashboard reflexes.

rules.name: The Tonal Step Rule
rules.body: Exactly three tonal steps exist for surfaces: page (#0a0a0f), card (#0f1117), inset/border (#1a1f2e). Nesting four levels of tonal step is forbidden — if a panel needs to feel deeper than its container, redesign the hierarchy.

dos: Do keep page backgrounds at Midnight Stage (#0a0a0f) — flat, no gradient, no vignette. The room is the room.
Do use tabular-nums on every live-updating digit. Timer, score, rank, count, participant total, points awarded.
Do reserve A/B/C/D option colours (#2563eb, #7c3aed, #d97706, #e11d48) for answer choices and nothing else.
Do apply one accent at a time per view (the Spotlight Rule). If two saturated colours are competing, redesign.
Do use hairline 1px Rule Line borders as the default structural device.
Do preserve the btn-lock-in-pending interaction — full opacity, cursor: wait, Framer overlay inside the button. It's the most important micro-state in the product.
Do use Framer's enterAnimation (opacity + 8px slide-up over 200ms) for new content arriving on screen.
Do keep Geist Mono inside form fields and code identifiers only.
Do respect prefers-reduced-motion — the global CSS rule is in place; don't bypass it with !important in component code.
donts: Don't look like Kahoot. No bright primaries on white, no confetti, no cartoon shapes. Hermes is staged, not slapstick.
Don't look like a generic SaaS dashboard. No rounded cards (radius stays 0), no soft gradient hero panels, no gray-on-color body text. Sharp corners, near-black canvas, signal-white text.
Don't drift into retro CRT kitsch. No scanlines, phosphor bloom, fake noise, or vintage-monitor curvature. The terminal is the soul — monospace inputs, uppercase tracked labels — not a costume.
Don't chase crypto / gaming neon. No cyan-on-black glow, no rainbow gradient text, no neon stroke effects. Saturated accents on flat dark, never light-leaks or bloom.
Don't use box-shadow on surfaces. Depth comes from the three tonal steps and from hairline borders. Drop shadows are a SaaS reflex.
Don't use gradient text. background-clip: text with a gradient fill is forbidden. Emphasis uses weight and size.
Don't nest a surface-card inside a surface-card. Re-architect the hierarchy.
Don't use Geist Mono for display numerals. It was not designed as a display face and breaks at large sizes — Geist Sans 900 with tabular-nums is the correct display digit.
Don't use light mode. Hermes is dark mode only by product doctrine; the token system supports a future light theme behind [data-theme] but no surface should be authored for light right now.
Don't introduce raw hex values in components. All colour goes through semantic tokens (var(--color-*) in CSS, colors.* in lib/design-tokens.ts).
Don't animate for decoration. Every motion must communicate a state change. If it could be removed without the user losing information, remove it.
